title: Cyrtandra gillespieana
intro: Cyrtandra gillespieana, also known as Gillespie's Cyrtandra, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtandra gillespieana include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbal medicine, Cyrtandra gillespieana is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Cyrtandra gillespieana is classified within the family Gesneriaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes mint, basil, and honeysuckle. The order Lamiales is part of the clade Asterids, which is a group of flowering plants thought to have originated over 100 million years ago. The clade Asterids is part of the larger clade Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. The classification of Cyrtandra gillespieana is further broken down to the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants.
